findings.evidence: Lines 122-124 introduce `R` only for `baseChange_antipode_tmul`, but the statement at lines 129-131 mentions only `k K A`; the roadmap target is `K ⊗[k] A` as a Hopf algebra over `K` (`TauCetiRoadmap/ReductiveGroups/README.md:124`).
findings.fix: Split the variable block and state `baseChange_antipode_tmul` only with `k K A`, `[CommSemiring k] [CommSemiring K] [Semiring A] [Algebra k K] [_root_.HopfAlgebra k A]`; keep the `R` assumptions only on the pointwise inverse lemmas.
findings.issue: `HopfAlgebra.baseChange_antipode_tmul` carries unused `R` assumptions (`[CommSemiring R] [Algebra K R] [Algebra k R] [IsScalarTower k K R]`), so the public Hopf base-change API cannot be used unless a value algebra for points is also supplied.

summary: The new antipode lemma exposes the right formula but with an unnecessarily narrow public type, tying a Hopf-algebra base-change fact to an unrelated points target algebra.
